steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The second sentence explains that sculptures are “moved from their original context, making precise dating impossible.” This directly characterizes what the assigned dates must be.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
The blank must describe dates that cannot be precise because sculptures have been moved from their original context. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — Uncontroversial: not causing disagreement — the dates being undisputed contradicts the passage's point that precise dating is impossible; controversy isn't the issue, accuracy is. ✘
  • Choice B — Irrelevant: not connected to the matter at hand — if the dates were irrelevant, archaeologists wouldn't bother assigning them; the passage says they assign dates, just imprecise ones. ✘
  • Choice C — Applicable: relevant and suitable — this says the dates apply, but doesn't address why they're necessarily imprecise; it doesn't follow from "precise dating impossible." ✘
  • Choice D — Approximate: roughly correct, not exact — directly follows from "precise dating impossible"; the dates are estimates, not pinpointed figures. ✔
That matches choice D — D is correct.
